ogTitle: Redford vs Redhill Farms: Climate Safety
ogDescription: Data-driven comparison of climate risk in Redford and Redhill Farms.
heading: Redford vs Redhill Farms — Which is Safer?
intro: Compare climate risk between Redford, Queensland and Redhill Farms, Queensland. Our analysis uses data from BOM, CSIRO, and state hazard authorities to score each suburb across 6 climate hazards.
verdict: Redhill Farms has a lower overall climate risk score (30/100) compared to Redford (34/100). We recommend getting an address-level report for properties in both suburbs before making a purchase decision.

faq.question: Is Redford or Redhill Farms safer from flooding?
faq.answer: Redford has a flood risk score of 34/100, while Redhill Farms scores 31/100. A lower score indicates lower flood risk. Get an address-level flood risk report for a specific property.

faq.question: Which suburb has lower bushfire risk?
faq.answer: Redford scores 55/100 for bushfire risk. Redhill Farms scores 19/100. Fire risk depends on proximity to vegetation and local fire history.

faq.question: Should I buy in Redford or Redhill Farms?
faq.answer: Both suburbs have climate considerations. Redhill Farms has a lower overall climate risk score. We recommend getting a full ClimateNest report for any specific property you're considering, which includes 8 hazard scores, insurance projections, and actionable recommendations.

valueImpact.description: Properties in this area may face a 0.53% climate value drag over time due to rising insurance costs and climate adaptation expenses.
